meaning: ADC stands for Analog-to-Digital Converter. It is a device that changes an analog signal into digital data.
An Analog-to-Digital Converter is a device that changes an analog signal into digital values so that a computer can use it.
A correlation coefficient shows how similar the shapes or patterns of two signals are. It does not mainly show the size of the error.

word: Difference Between RMSE and Correlation Coefficient
meaning: RMSE shows how large the numerical error is. The correlation coefficient shows how similar the signal shapes are.
Digital means data that is represented by separate numbers. Computers can store, process, and send digital data.
Digitalization is the process of changing a continuous analog signal into digital data. This makes the signal usable by computers or BCI systems.
Fire means that a neuron produces an action potential when its membrane potential goes over a threshold.
IrOx is iridium oxide. Electrodes coated with this material form an iridium oxide film on the surface, making the electrodes highly durable.
Neural firing is the event in which a neuron produces an action potential after its membrane potential passes a threshold.
PEDOT:PSS is a conductive polymer. It has high conductivity and can keep stable conductivity even under conditions such as humidity.
Quantization is the process of changing a continuous value into one of a limited number of digital levels.
RMSE stands for Root Mean Square Error. It shows the size of the numerical difference between two signals. A smaller RMSE means better accuracy.
Root Mean Square Error is a measure of the error between two signals. If the two signals are exactly the same, RMSE is 0.
Sampling means measuring an analog signal at regular time intervals.
A spike is a short electrical pulse produced by a neuron. It represents an important firing event.
Thermal noise voltage is noise caused by the random thermal motion of free electrons in a resistor. The noise voltage increases with the square root of the impedance.
Impedance is the resistance of a circuit or material to an alternating current. It includes both resistance and reactance.
EIS stands for Electrochemical Impedance Spectroscopy. It is a method used to measure the impedance of an electrochemical system over different frequencies.
Acquisition means the process of collecting or recording data. In neural engineering, it often means recording neural signals from the brain or nerves.
Attenuation means a decrease in signal strength. In neural signals, attenuation can happen when the signal becomes weaker during recording or transmission.
Contamination means the unwanted presence of foreign material or substances. In neural recording, contamination can reduce signal quality or affect the electrode surface.
Degradation means a decrease in quality or performance over time. For electrodes, it can mean that the electrode becomes less stable or less effective after use.
An electrode is a device used to record electrical signals or deliver electrical stimulation. In neural systems, electrodes can record signals from neurons or stimulate neural tissue.
Execution latency means the delay between giving a command and completing the action. In BCI systems, it can mean the delay between detecting a neural signal and producing the output command.
Iridium-oxide is a material used to coat electrodes. It can improve electrode durability and is useful for neural recording and stimulation.
Misinterpretation means understanding or classifying something incorrectly. In BCI systems, it can mean that a neural signal is decoded as the wrong command.
Noise is unwanted signal activity that interferes with the target neural signal. It can come from the body, the recording device, or the environment.
Tissue is a group of similar cells that work together. In neural engineering, it often refers to brain or nerve tissue around an electrode.
